citations.description: _ALERTING_DRIFT_CLASSES shows the fix is a reclassification, not per-item triage
Ticket comment from Joe Oakhart (last comment): claims 'NO actionable drift to remediate; the 176 are working-as-designed' but this is an untagged criterion requiring codebase evidence.

findings.criterion: Each reported drift is triaged (would_terminal → local archive/delete to propagate; dangling → confirmed 404 GC; unbound_jira → adopt or archive the Jira-native issue; local_gone → investigate).
findings.detail: This criterion requires individual, per-item operational triage actions (GC confirmations, Jira adoptions/archives, local investigations). The codebase shows only a classifier fix that reclassifies windowed-absence entries from 'dangling' into the new informational 'absent_in_window_unprobed' bucket. There is no code-visible evidence that any drift item was individually triaged per the stated remediation paths. The ticket comment from Joe Oakhart claims triage was done ('NO actionable drift to remediate; the 176 are working-as-designed'), but this is an untagged codebase-verifiable criterion and a ticket comment alone does not satisfy the codebase bar.
findings.remediation: If this triage was genuinely operational work (checking live Jira state, confirming no actual 404s needed, confirming no unbound_jira to adopt), tag the criterion as [operator-attested] and record a concrete attestation (named actor, timestamp, observed outcome per drift class). If code-verifiable triage actions were taken (e.g., local archives propagated), point to the relevant store changes.

citations.description: _empty_binding_drift() shows the 'empty' state is a dict with 6 keys, never truly empty
Windowed-absent entries are placed in absent_in_window_unprobed inside binding_drift, making the section non-empty
JSON output always includes the full binding_drift dict

Ticket comment: 'The literal AC empty binding_drift section was proven unsatisfiable under the OLD conflation... with the fix the ALERTING binding_drift is empty and only the informational bucket is populated.' This acknowledges the criterion is not literally met.

findings.detail: The code never produces an empty binding_drift section. Even in the 'clean' state, `_empty_binding_drift()` returns a dict with 6 keys all set to empty lists. With the fix applied, the 176 previously-false 'dangling' entries are routed to 'absent_in_window_unprobed' within the binding_drift dict, so binding_drift is non-empty (176 entries in the informational bucket). The fix correctly removes them from the alerting path (they no longer contribute to drift_total or the exit code), but the binding_drift section itself is not empty as the criterion literally requires. The JSON output at line 570 always emits the full binding_drift structure.
findings.remediation: Either (a) update the acceptance criterion to reflect what the fix actually achieves ('alerting binding_drift classes are empty') and re-scope the ticket, or (b) await a canary run confirming zero dangling/absent entries in all alerting classes and record that run URL as a ticket comment.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
